Document:
# Preamble NanoTrasen mandates that members of [color=red][bold]Security[/bold][/color] and [color=yellow][bold]Command[/bold][/color] personnel operate under an abbreviated Space Law. Below is a written list of clauses laid out as a series of bullet points that may readily and rapidly be referenced by number.
The exact meaning of terms may be referenced in the Definitions page. Terms with a listed definition will be marked in [color=#7af][bold]blue and bold[/bold][/color]
Failure to follow Space Law on duty constitutes as a [bold]3-04 - Neglect of Law[/bold] criminal charge, and is most likely to be enforced by adherent members of [color=red]Security[/color], if, of course, possible or reasonable in the moment.
# Space Law
## 1. Treatment of Prisoners
- 1.1. Prisoners must be granted adequate medical care in confinement. - - 1.1.1. Prisoners must have their corporeal wounds treated before or during confinement. - - 1.1.2. Prisoners must have access to medical equipment necessary for survival, ergo N2 internals gear granted to Vox. - - 1.1.3. Prisoners must be granted disability aids while in confinement, ergo canes granted to mobility impaired persons.
- 1.2. Prisoners must be granted adequate sustenance (food & water). - - 1.2.1. Sustenance granted to inmates must be consumable and digestible by the inmate, ergo allicin or theobromine free food granted to allergic species, cloth or fiber granted to Moth People.
- 1.3. Prisoners must not be [color=#7af][bold]restrained[/bold][/color] while in confinement, with the following exceptions. - - 1.3.1. Prisoners may be temporarily restrained when being relocated between confinement areas. - - 1.3.2. Prisoners may be temporarily restrained to mitigate risk to life or limb of other persons or restrained prisoner. - - 1.3.3. Prisoners may be temporarily restrained if the confinement area is unable to contain the prisoner, due to structural damage or otherwise.
- 1.4. Prisoners must be granted access to working headsets while in confinement. - - 1.4.1. Headsets confirmed to contain contraband radio keys may be confiscated and returned with the keys removed, or replaced immediately. - - 1.4.2. Abuse of communications may be acted upon by temporary confiscation of headset until end of confinement.
- 1.5. Prisoners must be held in a safe environment with no undue risk to life or limb. - - 1.5.1. If Genpop is damaged or becomes uninhabitable, confinement must be established elsewhere and repairs be made immediately. - - 1.5.2.
- 1.6. Prisoners must be granted adequate clothing while in confinement. - 1.6.1. Clothing rejected by prisoners for practical or preferential reasons must be provided with alternatives - 1.6.2 Uniforms and shoes must be available to prisoners at all times. Outer clothing or head garments may be provided on request. - 1.6.3. Clothing items may not be forced onto prisoners by other persons if the prisoner rejects them.
- 1.7. Non-contraband items must be returned to prisoners after confinement is concluded - 1.7.1 Non-contraband items used in the crime leading to arrest and sentence may be confiscated indefinitely. - 1.7.2. Essential items such as PDAs or job critical equipment must be returned or replaced after confiscation.
## 2. Apprehension and Escalation
- 2.1.
